nodes.name: Cron Trigger (every 5s)
nodes.notes: Polls every 5 seconds for pending queue items. Uses n8n Interval trigger — install the n8n-nodes-base interval package if not available, or replace with Cron (every minute) and process up to batch_size items per run.

nodes.name: Fan Out (batch)
nodes.notes: Creates batch_size parallel items so multiple queue items can be claimed in one workflow run. Adjust batch_size to match throughput needs.

nodes.name: Claim Next Message
nodes.notes: Calls claim_next_message(2) RPC. Returns the claimed queue ID or null. The function atomically picks the next pending message ordered by priority desc, created_at asc, and locks it to prevent duplicate processing.

nodes.name: Claimed?
nodes.notes: Checks if claim_next_message returned a valid integer ID. If null, no pending messages — exit early.

nodes.name: Extract Queue ID
nodes.notes: Normalizes the RPC response (may be a number directly or wrapped in { id }). Builds _queue_id for downstream nodes.

nodes.name: Fetch Queue Record
nodes.notes: Fetches queue item with join to stores, orders, and templates to get all data needed for sending.

nodes.name: Record Found?
nodes.notes: Ensures queue record and store data were successfully fetched.

nodes.name: Build Provider Payload
nodes.notes: Rebuilds provider payload from stored queue data. First tries to use stored payload; falls back to rebuilding from template + variables. Supports all 4 providers and all message types.

nodes.name: Send via Provider
nodes.notes: Sends the message via the provider. URL, headers, and body are fully dynamic from _provider_payload. continueOnFail enabled so errors are captured.

nodes.name: Parse Provider Response
nodes.notes: Parses provider response. Extracts message ID on success, error details on failure.

nodes.name: Send Successful?
nodes.notes: Routes success vs failure to appropriate update paths.

nodes.name: Update Queue Sent
nodes.notes: Marks queue item as 'sent' and releases lock.

nodes.name: Update Log Sent
nodes.notes: Updates message_logs with sent status and provider message ID.

nodes.name: Update Orders Sent
nodes.notes: Updates orders table with provider_message_id and sent_at timestamp.

nodes.name: Refresh Metrics (success)
nodes.notes: Refreshes metrics cache after successful send.

nodes.name: Calculate Backoff
nodes.notes: Calculates exponential backoff: attempt 1→5s, 2→15s, 3→30s, 4→60s, 5→300s. After max retries, marks as failed permanently.

nodes.name: Max Retries Reached?
nodes.notes: If max retries reached, mark as permanently failed. Otherwise, schedule retry with backoff.

nodes.name: Update Queue Failed
nodes.notes: Marks queue item as failed permanently.

nodes.name: Update Log Failed
nodes.notes: Updates message_logs with failed status and error.

nodes.name: Update Orders Failed
nodes.notes: Updates orders table with failed status.

nodes.name: Update Queue Retry
nodes.notes: Updates queue item with incremented retry_count, next_retry_at for backoff, and last_error. Status set back to 'pending' so it can be picked up again by claim_next_message.

nodes.name: Update Log Retry
nodes.notes: Updates message_logs back to pending for retry.

nodes.name: Refresh Metrics
nodes.notes: Refreshes metrics cache after retry/failure update.
